formal_strategy_baseline: ONE_SHOT_DONCHIAN_ATR_BREAKOUT 1.0.0 on BTCUSDT-PERP; fixed background only
question: Does a continuously held equal-BTC-quantity spot-long/perpetual-short hedge earn positive, funding-dominated returns after conservative two-leg costs and a 4% annual capital hurdle across sequential BTC periods?
position: one BTC spot long plus one BTCUSDT USD-M perpetual short; equal BTC quantity; no rebalancing inside a phase
capital: spot purchase plus fully funded short initial notional; no leverage credit
development_gate: data PASS; base/stress total >0; stress return after 4% annual capital hurdle >0; 2021 and 2022 each base >0; max drawdown >-8%; funding PnL > absolute basis PnL
confirmation_gate: data PASS; base/stress total >0; stress after hurdle >0; max drawdown >-8%; funding dominates absolute basis; evaluation+confirmation noncompounded >0
conclusion_rule: SUPPORTS_WITHIN_SCOPE only after all sequential gates. Negative confirmation or combined return is DOES_NOT_SUPPORT; other failure is INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.
study_code_revision_reason: After development PnL but before evaluation release, label each 8h PnL by interval start rather than end so the final 2022 interval is not assigned to the end-exclusive 2023 boundary. PnL, rules, periods, costs and gates unchanged.
known_exposure: Underlying BTC data and event-triggered carry were exposed. Continuous always-on equal-quantity carry outcomes were not executed before this checkpoint; holdout is rule-level.
benchmarks: half-capital BTC spot long with identical round-trip cost
